comment_text.sn12.45:1.2: Ñātika (also spelled nātika, or nādika; Sanskrit jñātṛka; Prākrit nāyika) was the clan to which the Jain leader Mahāvīra (called Nāṭaputta, i.e. Ñātiputta) belonged. Here it is the name of their chief town.

root_text.sn12.45:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā ñātike viharati giñjakāvasathe.
root_text.sn12.45:1.3: Atha kho bhagavā rahogato paṭisallāno imaṁ dhammapariyāyaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn12.45:2.1: “Cakkhuñca paṭicca rūpe ca uppajjati cakkhuviññāṇaṁ. Tiṇṇaṁ saṅgati phasso.
root_text.sn12.45:3.5: manañca paṭicca dhamme ca uppajjati manoviññāṇaṁ. Tiṇṇaṁ saṅgati phasso.
root_text.sn12.45:4.1: Cakkhuñca paṭicca rūpe ca uppajjati cakkhuviññāṇaṁ. Tiṇṇaṁ saṅgati phasso.
root_text.sn12.45:5.2: manañca paṭicca dhamme ca uppajjati manoviññāṇaṁ. Tiṇṇaṁ saṅgati phasso.
root_text.sn12.45:6.1: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu bhagavato upassuti ṭhito hoti.
root_text.sn12.45:6.2: Addasā kho bhagavā taṁ bhikkhuṁ upassuti ṭhitaṁ.

translation_text.sn12.45:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying at Ñātika in the brick house.
translation_text.sn12.45:1.3: Then while the Buddha was in private retreat he spoke this exposition of the teaching:
translation_text.sn12.45:2.1: “Eye consciousness arises dependent on the eye and sights. The meeting of the three is contact.
translation_text.sn12.45:2.2: Contact is a requirement for feeling.
translation_text.sn12.45:2.3: Feeling is a requirement for craving.
translation_text.sn12.45:2.4: Craving is a requirement for grasping. …
translation_text.sn12.45:2.5: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.
translation_text.sn12.45:3.1: Ear consciousness arises dependent on the ear and sounds. …
translation_text.sn12.45:3.2: Nose consciousness arises dependent on the nose and smells. …
translation_text.sn12.45:3.3: Tongue consciousness arises dependent on the tongue and tastes. …
translation_text.sn12.45:3.4: Body consciousness arises dependent on the body and touches. …
translation_text.sn12.45:3.5: Mind consciousness arises dependent on the mind and ideas. The meeting of the three is contact.
translation_text.sn12.45:3.6: Contact is a requirement for feeling.
translation_text.sn12.45:3.7: Feeling is a requirement for craving.
translation_text.sn12.45:3.8: Craving is a requirement for grasping. …
translation_text.sn12.45:3.9: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.
translation_text.sn12.45:4.1: Eye consciousness arises dependent on the eye and sights. The meeting of the three is contact.
translation_text.sn12.45:4.2: Contact is a requirement for feeling.
translation_text.sn12.45:4.3: Feeling is a requirement for craving.
translation_text.sn12.45:4.4: When that craving fades away and ceases with nothing left over, grasping ceases.
translation_text.sn12.45:4.5: When grasping ceases, continued existence ceases. …
translation_text.sn12.45:4.6: That is how this entire mass of suffering ceases.
translation_text.sn12.45:5.1: Ear consciousness arises dependent on the ear and sounds. …
translation_text.sn12.45:5.2: Mind consciousness arises dependent on the mind and ideas. The meeting of the three is contact.
translation_text.sn12.45:5.3: Contact is a requirement for feeling.
translation_text.sn12.45:5.4: Feeling is a requirement for craving.
translation_text.sn12.45:5.5: When that craving fades away and ceases with nothing left over, grasping ceases.
translation_text.sn12.45:5.6: When grasping ceases, continued existence ceases. …
translation_text.sn12.45:5.7: That is how this entire mass of suffering ceases.”
translation_text.sn12.45:6.1: Now at that time a certain monk was standing listening in on the Buddha.
translation_text.sn12.45:6.4: “Monk, did you hear that exposition of the teaching?”
translation_text.sn12.45:6.9: That exposition of the teaching is beneficial and relevant to the fundamentals of the spiritual life.”

variant_text.sn12.45:6.9: Atthasaṁhito ayaṁ → atthasaṁhitoyaṁ (bj); atthasañhitoyaṁ (sya-all, km); atthasaṁhitāyaṁ (pts1ed, pts2ed, mr)
